0.q: Phytoene synthase (PSY), an essential catalytic enzyme in the process, regulates this crucial step in the pathway; ectopic expression of DcPSY2 produces a significant increase in total carotenoids.
Phytoene synthase (PSY) is one of the key enzymes in carotenoid and ABA biosynthesis. It is DcPSY2 which shows the highest increase in transcript levels under salt and ABA treatments.

1.q: the mutant strain transformed with pET-Blue1/DcLCYB2 plasmid presented three peaks, two of which corresponds to lycopene and β-carotene, and a third peak that corresponds to γ-carotene, a monocyclic intermediary in the conversion of lycopene to β-carotene.

2.q: All Al4 transgenic lines...an increase in total carotenoids and chlorophyll a and b compared to empty vector (EV) control lines was also observed.

3.q: The S. cerevisiae Y1HGol-pP2-AbAi reporter strain transformed with vectors carrying DcAL4 and DcAL7 fused to the Gal4 AD survived in the presence of AbAi, suggesting that DcAL4 and DcAL7 bind to the DcPSY2 promoter.

4.q: The S. cerevisiae Y1HGol-pP2-AbAi reporter strain transformed with vectors carrying DcAL4 and DcAL7 fused to the Gal4 AD survived in the presence of AbAi, suggesting that DcAL4 and DcAL7 bind to the DcPSY2 promoter.

5.q: BADH enzyme activity was enhanced 8-fold in transgenic carrot cell cultures, grew 7-fold more, and accumulated 50- to 54-fold more betaine (93–101 μmol g−1 dry weight of β-Ala betaine and Gly betaine) than untransformed cells.

6.q: ectopic expression of DcPSY2 in Nicotiana tabacum produces in L3 and L6 a significant increase in total carotenoids and chlorophyll a, and a significant increment in phytoene in the T1 L6 line.

7.q: ectopic expression of DcPSY2 in Nicotiana tabacum produces in L3 and L6 a significant increase in total carotenoids and chlorophyll a, and a significant increment in phytoene in the T1 L6 line.

8.q: DcPSY2 T1 transgenic tobacco lines exhibit an increased survival rate after salt treatment... directly associated with the upregulation of abiotic-related gene expression and ABA levels in roots.

9.q: The predicted proteins DcPSY1 (398 aa) and DcPSY2 (437 aa) share 64% identity and present the same amino acids at the active site, substrate binding pocket, Mg2+ binding site and the squalene/phytoene synthase domains.

10.q: In carrot... DcPSY1 and DcPSY2 paralogs have been identified. The predicted proteins DcPSY1 (398 aa) and DcPSY2 (437 aa) share 64% identity.

11.q: These results showed that Hsp17.7 from carrot can function as a molecular chaperone increasing soluble protein levels in stressed transgenic yeast cells.
